title: Meibomia adscendens
intro: Meibomia adscendens, also known as Purple Coneflower Meibomia, is a perennial legume native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Meibomia adscendens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Meibomia adscendens is often prepared as a tincture or infused in teas, and it is also used in topical applications, such as salves and creams, for its skin-soothing properties.
taxonomy: Meibomia adscendens is classified as a species within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Desmodieae. This classification places Meibomia adscendens within the larger group of pea-like plants. The genus Meibomia is native to North America and is often used as a source of medicinal compounds. Meibomia adscendens is further classified as a perennial herb, characterized by its woody base and branching stems.
uses: Meibomia adscendens, also known as butterfly pea, is traditionally used in various folk remedies for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In some cultures, Meibomia adscendens is used to treat respiratory issues like coughs and colds due to its expectorant properties. Additionally, the plant is thought to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to boost the immune system. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

parts: Meibomia adscendens, which is also known as the butterfly pea or gentian, is a plant used in traditional medicine. The medicinal parts of the plant are its leaves and roots, which are used to treat various health conditions. The leaves are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, while the roots are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
